tldr: Three of your five live ads are running the identical 'Shop New Arrivals / ZII ROPA SS26' lockup pointing to the same New Women collection — that's a frequency bomb that'll spike CPMs inside 7-10 days. The two event ads (Roma Norte flagship, Williamsburg pop-up) are doing different work but neither is carrying the craft story that's actually your moat.

benchmark.context: Sustainable fashion brands in your tier typically rotate 6-8 distinct creative angles at once. You're effectively running two: 'new arrivals' (3x) and 'come visit us' (2x). The Japanese fabrics + Made in Mexico City line only appears once across all five ads — that's the story competitors can't copy.

ads.headline: Shop New Arrivals
drivers: Body copy is just two words — 'ZII ROPA SS26' — which gives the algorithm nothing to optimize against and tells your buyer nothing about why to click
Identical headline + body + CTA + landing URL as two other live ads, so Meta is bidding against itself in the same auction
No mention of fabric, craft, or what 'SS26' actually contains — the conscious-consumer hook is missing entirely

ads.headline: Shop New Arrivals
ads.body: ZII ROPA SS26  Japanese fabrics. Made in Mexico City.
drivers: This is the strongest of the three 'New Arrivals' variants because it actually names the craft story — but it's drowning because the other two duplicates are eating its impressions
Same headline and CTA as two sibling ads, so creative differentiation lives entirely in the body — fragile
The 'Japanese fabrics. Made in Mexico City.' line deserves to be the headline, not buried after a SKU code

ads.body: Williamsburg, this week only ZII ROPA + ODISSEA pop-up 135 Wythe Ave April 30 – May 4 11–7PM
drivers: No headline on this ad — the body is doing all the work, and Meta typically truncates after ~125 characters in feed, so 'April 30 – May 4 11–7PM' may not even render
Pop-up ends May 4 — this ad has 4 days of useful life left and then becomes a wasted impression generator unless someone pauses it manually
Landing URL is a share.google redirect, which Meta's quality score penalizes and conscious-consumer buyers don't trust

hero_concept.hook: Cut once. Worn for a decade.
hero_concept.headline: The LAURENT Pant
hero_concept.primary_text:
Caroline sources the linen from a third-generation mill outside Kyoto. Then it travels to a small atelier in Roma Norte, where four people cut and stitch every LAURENT pant by hand.

That's why the drape sits the way it does — and why the seams will still be square after a hundred wears. Limited run. When this linen is gone, it's gone.

Shop SS26 at ziiropa.com.
hero_concept.visual_direction: The LAURENT pant photographed on a soft natural backdrop in warm Mexico City afternoon light, with subtle texture detail showing the linen weave.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Three of your five live ads are running the same 'Shop New Arrivals / SS26' lockup with no story attached — the craft narrative (Japanese fabrics, Made in Mexico City) only appears as a buried tag on one variant. This concept makes that craft story the entire ad and gives a single hero SKU to anchor it.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a quiet Roma Norte atelier setting with the pant displayed against soft linen and natural wood surfaces, with warm late-afternoon Mexico City daylight raking across the fabric to reveal the weave texture. Muted, earthy palette — bone, sand, soft terracotta — with a calm, intentional, slow-fashion editorial mood that signals craft and longevity. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. Maintain the exact appearance, color, and details of the product from the reference image. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no text, no logos, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no buttons, no faces, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products.

next_step.urgency: Three duplicate ads hit hard fatigue inside 7-9 days.
next_step.headline: 15 minutes to map your SS26 rotation
next_step.body: I'll walk you through which of the three 'New Arrivals' duplicates to kill today and how to rebuild around the Japanese-fabric story. We can also queue a replacement for the Williamsburg pop-up before May 4.

method_note: Signals scored from publicly visible Meta Ad Library creative — frequency and CPM modeled, not measured from your ad account.
